commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `sessions_send` accept `SendMessage`, `content`, and `text` aliases, normalizes the first non-empty alias into `message`, and updates regression plus prompt snapshot coverage.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +63. Total +75 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and `v2026.5.27` require `message` in the sessions-send schema and read it as required before dispatch, so a `SendMessage`-only call is source-reproducible as a validation/execution rejection.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize sessions_send message aliases
target.body:
## Summary

- Allow `sessions_send` / `send_to_task` to accept model-generated body aliases: `SendMessage`, `content`, and `text`.
- Normalize the first non-empty alias into the canonical `message` field before dispatching the agent run.
- Add regression coverage for the schema shape, alias execution path, and prompt snapshots.

Closes #88146.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A `sessions_send` tool call with `SendMessage` now passes the real tool schema boundary and is normalized into the outgoing agent `message` payload instead of being rejected for a missing `message` property.
- **Real environment tested:** Patched task worktree with an isolated OpenClaw dev gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:19081` and isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-dev-3702711`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Started the dev gateway from the patched worktree; ran `node --import tsx $EVIDENCE_DIR/sessions-send-alias-proof-current.ts`, which imports the patched `createSessionsSendTool`, calls it with `SendMessage` and no canonical `message`, and sends through the real gateway via `src/gateway/call.ts`; ran the targeted Vitest and TS checks listed below.
- **Evidence after fix:** `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88146-evidence/openclaw-real-behavior-proof.md`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88146-evidence/sessions-send-alias-proof-current.json`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88146-evidence/openclaw-start.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88146-evidence/openclaw-dev-gateway.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88146-evidence/vitest-sessions-send.txt`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88146-evidence/tsgo-core-test-current.log`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Runtime proof reports `schemaAllowsMissingMessage: true`, `schemaHasSendMessageAlias: true`, gateway methods `sessions.resolve`, `sessions.create`, and `agent`, final status `accepted`, session key `agent:main:main`, and `runIdPresent: true`.
- **What was not tested:** A live Anthropic/Pi model naturally choosing the alias was not tested; the proof injects the rejected `SendMessage` shape from the issue and verifies the patched tool schema and dispatch boundary against a real running gateway.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** #84079 added alias normalization for the channel `message` tool, but `sessions_send` still required the canonical `message` property at its own schema boundary. Anthropic/Pi-backed agents can emit the body as `SendMessage`, so those calls failed validation before `sessions_send` could execute. The schema now admits the common aliases and the execution path canonicalizes them before reading `message` as required.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
